title: Causal tyre-cliff trend features
summary: Inference from F1/AWS tyre-performance notes: stop decisions depend on effective tyre performance being used up, not just raw tyre age.
rationale: Inference from F1/AWS tyre-performance notes: stop decisions depend on effective tyre performance being used up, not just raw tyre age. Public pit-stop ML work also reports tyre age and recent pace as dominant signals. The current best features are mostly static and miss short-horizon deterioration dynamics.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year`-`Driver` sequence sorted by lap, build strictly lagged 1/2/3-lap deltas, rolling slopes, accelerations, EWMAs, and shock flags for `LapTime_Delta`, `LapTime (s)`, `Cumulative_Degradation`, and `Position_Change`. Reset rolling features after `PitStop==1`, and add demeaned versions versus same-race medians to reduce track-level noise.
expected_effect: Better separation between ordinary old-tyre laps and genuine imminent-stop laps, especially when fixed compound-life windows are noisy.
risk: High sensitivity to safety-car/red-flag/noise laps; leakage occurs if any rolling window touches the current or future lap incorrectly.
